Hacker News Confidence

Less annoying hacker news. (What is this?)

This site scrapes Hacker News once every 30 minutes, then sorts according to this formula, with upvotes as positive signals and comments as negative ones. In my experience, the best articles to read on HN are the ones with a high upvote-to-discussion ratio. Mostly because controversial pieces tend to produce a disproportionate number of comments compared to upvotes.


  1. WolfIP: Lightweight TCP/IP stack with no dynamic memory allocations 56 upvotes, 5 comments
  2. Dolphin Progress Release 2603 257 upvotes, 41 comments
  3. DDR4 Sdram – Initialization, Training and Calibration 32 upvotes, 3 comments
  4. The Met Releases High-Def 3D Scans of 140 Famous Art Objects 140 upvotes, 30 comments
  5. Italian prosecutors seek trial for Amazon, 4 execs in alleged $1.4B tax evasion 185 upvotes, 51 comments
  6. Reversing memory loss via gut-brain communication 118 upvotes, 30 comments
  7. Long Overlooked as Crucial to Life, Fungi Start to Get Their Due 56 upvotes, 12 comments
  8. Malus – Clean Room as a Service 798 upvotes, 314 comments
  9. Show HN: Understudy – Teach a desktop agent by demonstrating a task once 46 upvotes, 11 comments
  10. Bubble Sorted Amen Break 158 upvotes, 58 comments
  11. Show HN: OneCLI – Vault for AI Agents in Rust 82 upvotes, 29 comments
  12. The Emotional Labor Behind AI Intimacy (2025) [pdf] 38 upvotes, 12 comments
  13. Apple's MacBook Neo makes repairs easier and cheaper than other MacBooks 122 upvotes, 57 comments
  14. Full Spectrum and Infrared Photography 31 upvotes, 11 comments
  15. The Cost of Indirection in Rust 60 upvotes, 27 comments
  16. An old photo of a large BBS 45 upvotes, 19 comments
  17. White House plan to break up iconic U.S. climate lab moves forward 97 upvotes, 50 comments
  18. Launch HN: IonRouter (YC W26) – High-throughput, low-cost inference 22 upvotes, 7 comments
  19. Show HN: Web-based ANSI art viewer 18 upvotes, 6 comments
  20. Show HN: Rudel – Claude Code Session Analytics 116 upvotes, 71 comments
  21. US banks' exposure to private credit hits $300B (2025) 208 upvotes, 140 comments
  22. Asia rolls out 4-day weeks, WFH to solve fuel crisis caused by Iran war 341 upvotes, 258 comments
  23. Runners Are Discovering It's Surprisingly Easy to Churn Butter on Their Runs 4 upvotes, 0 comments
  24. Kotlin creator's new language: talk to LLMs in specs, not English 241 upvotes, 198 comments
  25. The Biggest Identity Sandpiles and How to Compute Them 15 upvotes, 6 comments
  26. NaN Is Weird 15 upvotes, 6 comments
  27. Show HN: Axe – A 12MB binary that replaces your AI framework 93 upvotes, 69 comments
  28. ATMs didn't kill bank teller jobs, but the iPhone did 212 upvotes, 257 comments
  29. Contextual commits – An open standard for capturing the why in Git history 22 upvotes, 21 comments
  30. Converge (YC S23) Is Hiring a Founding Platform Engineer (NYC, Onsite) 0 upvotes, 0 comments

Last generated 4:30am, 13 March 2026. Source here.
